title: storage-chown-by-maps child of `podman run --uidmap=10001:1001:1 myimage /bin/ls` hangs for 2 minutes on first launch
body:
### Issue Description

Running container image based on opensuse/leap:15.6 hangs for a few minutes.
While the `podman run` command is running, parallel invocation of `podman images` will also hang

May be related: #14989, which was closed without solution.


### Steps to reproduce the issue


1. Create `src` directory with multiple files - the more, the longer it hangs
```bash
> mkdir src
> touch src/{{a..z},{A..Z},{0..99}}.txt
```

1. Create Dockerfile based on opensuse/leap:15.6:

```
from opensuse/leap:15.6 as base

ENV UID=9999
ENV GID=9999

RUN groupadd -g $GID ufo_docker_group || exit 0
RUN useradd --shell /bin/bash -u $UID -g $GID -o -c "" -m ufo_docker_user -l || exit 0
ENV DOCKER_USER=ufo_docker_user

USER $UID:$GID

COPY --chown=$UID:$GID src /project/

CMD ["/bin/ls", "-l"]
```

1. Build the image: `podman build --tag mysuse .`
1. Run the built image: `podman --log-level debug run --uidmap=9998:1001:1   --gidmap=9998:1001:1 --uidmap=0:0:1 --gidmap=0:0:1 mysuse /bin/ls -l`
`
NOTE: intentionally set --uidmap and --gidmap to wrong number, 9998 instead of correct value used by container (9999)
1. Notice the above command takes 30+  seconds to complete (or few minutes, if created ~1500 files)
1. While the command above still runnig, open another console, and try to run `podman images`
1. Notice that `podman images` command also hangs until `podman run` is complete

1. Notice that after the first invocation of  `podman --log-level debug run --uidmap=9998:1001:1   --gidmap=9998:1001:1 --uidmap=0:0:1 --gidmap=0:0:1 mysuse /bin/ls -l`, repeating that command will complete almost immediately

### Describe the results you received

First invocation of `podman --log-level debug run --uidmap=9998:1001:1   --gidmap=9998:1001:1 --uidmap=0:0:1 --gidmap=0:0:1 mysuse /bin/ls -l` command takes few minutes to complete.

Podman is waiting on child process `storage-chown-by-maps /home/u1/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/c847eafd877dd839576da128d736534ab2d352b9486444e1435f13cfcf23cff3/merged`

After few minutes, the log output is as expected, shows the error about UID 9999 not mapped:


First invocation of `podman run` completes in a few seconds, not minutes

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

NOTE: alpine container have no issues, only opensuse hangs for long time

comments.body:
If you use a custom user namesapce the storage has to copy the entire image with the new id mappings to ensure the file uids for the container will be correct. That will always be a slow operation if the image is big.

However once the image was copied/chowned then it is already in storage so the next run is fast see https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/performance.md#choosing-a-storage-driver

But why the issue only happens on image based on opensuse, and does not happen on alpine?

And why it pwns parallel command `podman images`?
How big is the image? Writing the files will take time. I assume alpine is juts much smaller? And/or you already have the right idmapped alpine image in the store

Ah, i see now, thanks. The image size matters here.

opensuse 117 MB
alpine 8.29 MB

```
> podman images
REPOSITORY                           TAG               IMAGE ID      CREATED             SIZE
localhost/mysuse                     latest            bbeb674d0653  About a minute ago  117 MB
localhost/myalpine                   latest            cb49af86f189  2 hours ago         8.29 MB
```
